title: Police certificate: How to get a police certificate - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate
In most cases, you must contact the local police or government to ask for a certificate.
- provide information or documents like: photographs fingerprints the addresses where you lived in the country or territory and the dates that you lived there
- the addresses where you lived in the country or territory and the dates that you lived there
If your certificate is in a language other than English or French, send the original police certificate along with a translation from a certified translator .
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Get a police certificate
Find out how and where to get a police certificate from different countries.
Select a country or territory:
- Bosnia and Herzegovina
- British Virgin Islands
- Central African Republic
- China (People’s Republic of)
- Congo (Democratic Republic of)
- Denmark (including Faro Island and Greenland)
- Falkland Islands (Malvinas)
- Italy (including Vatican City)
- Micronesia (Federated States of)
- Montenegro (former Yugoslavia)
- North Macedonia (Republic of)
- Northern Mariana Islands
- Sao Tome and Principe
- Spain (including the Canary Islands)
- St. Vincent and the Grenadines
- Timor Leste (Timor oriental)
- Turks and Caicos Islands
- United Arab Emirates
- United States of America
Contact the national police agency, or your country or territory’s embassy or consulate in Canada.
- show proof that you requested a police certificate from the correct authorities
- write a letter explaining all you’ve done to try to get one
Even if you send us both of the above items, we can’t guarantee we’ll accept your application.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
